cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The mental processes of acquiring, storing, and using knowledge, including memory, thinking, and language

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The process of encoding, storing, and retrieving information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The process of converting information into a form that can be stored in memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The retention of encoded information over time

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The process of accessing stored information when needed

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A three-stage memory model: sensory memory → short-term memory → long-term memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Very brief memory that holds sensory information (about 0.5 seconds for vision, 3-4 seconds for hearing)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory that briefly holds about 7±2 items of information currently being processed

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Relatively permanent and unlimited memory store for facts, skills, and experiences

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A memory model that emphasizes active mental processing of information in a central executive with two slave systems

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory for facts and general knowledge (semantic) and personal events (episodic)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory for skills, habits, and conditioned responses; unconscious memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory for personal experiences and autobiographical events

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory for general facts and knowledge about the world

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The process of rehearsing items by repeating them to keep them in short-term memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Connecting new information to existing knowledge to encode it more deeply

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Grouping information into meaningful units to expand working memory capacity

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to recall items at the beginning of a list better due to rehearsal into long-term memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to recall items at the end of a list better because they remain in working memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The inability to form new long-term memories after brain damage; seen after hippocampal damage

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Loss of memory for events before brain damage or trauma

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Forgetting caused by interference from other memories (proactive or retroactive)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: When old memories interfere with learning and recalling new information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: When new learning interferes with recall of previously learned information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Better recall of information when in the same physical context in which it was learned

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Better recall when in the same emotional or physiological state as when information was encoded

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory cues linked to information that improve retrieval

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The inability to recall information that is stored but temporarily inaccessible — like knowing but not quite retrieving a word

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The distortion of memory by information received after an event occurred

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Elizabeth Loftus
cards.answer: Research on the misinformation effect and false memories; showed eyewitness testimony is malleable

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A mental framework or category that organizes and interprets information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The mental strategy or rule of thumb used to quickly solve problems or make judgments

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A step-by-step procedure that guarantees a correct solution if followed correctly

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The sudden realization of a solution to a problem; 'aha moment'

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Judging the likelihood of an event based on how easily an example comes to mind

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Judging whether something belongs to a category based on how closely it resembles the typical case

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to search for information that confirms existing beliefs while ignoring contradictory evidence

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The inability to see a problem from a new perspective; being stuck on one approach

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to use an object only in its traditional way, failing to see novel uses

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to overestimate the accuracy of one's own knowledge and predictions

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A human's system of communication using symbols (words) combined by rules (grammar)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The smallest units of sound in a language (b, p, t, a, etc.)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The smallest units of meaning in a language (words, prefixes, suffixes)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The rules of a language that govern how words and sentences are structured

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The theory that language shapes thought and perception; speakers of different languages perceive the world differently

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Noam Chomsky
cards.answer: Proposed nativist language theory — humans have an innate language acquisition device (LAD) enabling rapid language learning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Chomsky's proposed innate mechanism that enables children to rapidly acquire language rules

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The sensitive developmental period during which language is most easily and naturally acquired

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The ability to produce many ideas; a component of creative thinking

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Narrowing options to find the single correct solution; as opposed to divergent thinking

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Hermann Ebbinghaus
cards.answer: First systematic study of memory and forgetting; discovered the forgetting curve showing rapid initial memory decay

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A graphic showing the rapid initial loss of memory followed by a more gradual decline over time

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Spacing practice over time rather than massing it in a single session to improve retention

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Actively retrieving information from memory as a study strategy, rather than re-reading

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to believe, after learning the outcome, that you knew it all along

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory technique using vivid imagery and spatial locations (imagining items in a route)
